ticket.subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] FedEx Rest API - Upload Additional Documents Feature

ticket.description:
Hello Abraham,

The solution SatisfyRunning is using is the PH Multi-Carrier Shipping Label (PH MCSL) app for Shopify and I have shared the Keys below.

"l7c13184694b0f49c59beb7dbbf1e35082" (Shipping)
"l7db6ee082c3f74a4abe1c629bd2c0aeae" (Tracking)

I have also share the API keys mapped to other PluginHive solutions for your reference:

PH Ship, Rate & Track for FedEx / (Shopify FedEx):
'”l75b57e2561b5c4c13a864e7ab3ff4f73c” (Shipping)
“l7043871ca2af64e0380534d15d92eb25f” (Tracking)

FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label / (WooCommerce FedEx Shipping):
“l72cb2d45c40fc42258cecaac28473e9bf” (Shipping)
“l7f94e2534717749bab8dcb30938fca2e9” (Tracking)

WooCommerce Shipping Services:
”l7f3821226b8f84cb18e24f29f83161761” (Shipping)
“l709bd848cca44420cb52e67d5d16c7042” (Tracking)

Shopify Shipment Tracking & Notifications App:
“l7db6ee082c3f74a4abe1c629bd2c0aeae" (Tracking)

Please let me know if you need any further details.

Best regards,
Keerthi
Team PluginHive

Let me review that I’ll get back to you in a minute.
 
Abraham Gougam | Customer Solutions Specialist-Senior (ID) | IT Customer Integrations | FedEx Express, 5 All. des Bas Tilliers, 92230 Gennevilliers
 
From: Keerthi <keerthishree@pluginhive.com> 
Sent: Thursday, April 2, 2026 1:12 PM
To: Abraham Ait Gougam <aait-gougam@fedex.com>
Cc: Sofie Bertholdson <s.bertholdson@satisfyrunning.com>; Thibaut Dutartre <t.dutartre@satisfyrunning.com>; Stephane Grosperrin <sgrosperrin@fedex.com>; Veena <veena@pluginhive.com>
Subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] FedEx Rest API - Upload Additional Documents Feature
 
Hello Abraham, 
 
Good day!
 
This is Keerthi from PluginHive. 
 
SatisfyRunning (Sofie) is currently in the process of migrating their FedEx account to the REST API. 
 
However, when attempting to upload documents using the Auto Post - Upload Documents feature, they are encountering the following error: "Enterprise Document Upload Service Unavailable”.
 
I have attached the request and response files with this email for your reference. 
 
Could you please help us understand the cause of this issue and advise on how to resolve it? 
 
Additionally, I noticed in the image shared from your end, that the permissions show “TEST STATUS”. Could you please confirm if this means the feature is enabled only in the test environment? If so, could this be the reason for the error they are encountering?
 
Looking forward to your guidance.
 
Best regards,
Keerthi
Team PluginHive

Sofie
I have found that this account is linked to our partner Pluginhive as a Child user, and all below sub-APIs activated.
So for me everything is ON .

Best regards, 
Sofie
 
On Thu, 2 Apr 2026 at 12:08, Abraham Ait Gougam <aait-gougam@fedex.com> wrote:
Hi Sofie ,
 
I can check that for you, can you please share with me the FedEx account number you are using to use the FedEx API please?
 
I’ll be able to check if the Document Upload API is set .
 
Abraham Gougam | Customer Solutions Specialist-Senior (ID) | IT Customer Integrations | FedEx Express, 5 All. des Bas Tilliers, 92230 Gennevilliers
 
From: Sofie Bertholdson <s.bertholdson@satisfyrunning.com> 
Sent: Thursday, April 2, 2026 11:42 AM
To: Abraham Ait Gougam <aait-gougam@fedex.com>
Cc: Thibaut Dutartre <t.dutartre@satisfyrunning.com>; Stephane Grosperrin <sgrosperrin@fedex.com>
Subject: [EXTERNAL] FedEx Rest API - Upload Additional Documents Feature
 
Caution! This email originated outside of FedEx. Please do not open attachments or click links from an unknown or suspicious origin.
 
Hi Abraham,
 
Reaching out as we are in the midst of changing the FedEx API connection in our multicarrier app. 
 
While doing the switch we have run into some problems, one being unable to upload additional documents.
Can you confirm if the Auto Upload document option is enabled for our FedEx account through Rest API? 
 
Best regards, 
Sofie

comments.body:
Hello Keerthi



I have just check in our API backend system, the project named**Shopify Multicarrier API2023 Certification with**

TEST KEY : l741e31f58b8b9462ba38759b739c4f041

PROD KEY : l7c13184694b0f49c59beb7dbbf1e35082

Has Trade Documents Upload API ENABLED .



Can you please run a new test and share your logs again with me , no problem with the API on my side.

![](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/L2NjzhJ5EJc7Kb0n5lJdfpZ1T/?name=image001.png)

**Abraham Gougam****|** Customer Solutions Specialist-Senior (ID) | IT Customer Integrations| FedEx Express,5 All. des Bas Tilliers, 92230 Gennevilliers

Hello Abraham,

Thank you for confirming that the Trade Documents Upload API is enabled on your end.

We ran a new test order from our side, and observed that the **Auto Upload** (automatic document upload during label generation) is still failing. However, the **Post Upload** (manual document upload via the app) is working as expected.

As requested, we have shared the **request and response XML logs** from this test in the link below, as they are too large to include in the email:

**Request/Response XML**

Kindly review the logs and let us know your findings.

Regards,

Sibi Firoz
Team PluginHive

Hello Keerthi

This is Easter Monday in France and we are out of the office, I can review that again for you tomorrow.

comments.body:
Hello Veena,



Thank you for the payload, the error message is clear indeed.



I have checked again; the Trade Document Upload API is correctly activated in SANDBOX & PRODUCTION for MASTER ( PH ) and CHILD ( SATISFY)

as per account numbers and API Keys you have gently provided to me .

IN general, when it is not activated, the API replies “Credentials Not Authorozied”



Nonetheless, the submitted document has been correctly inserted in our imaging systems, so it means that it worked after all.

![](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/Aot8XY7BWfpTwLBu2n6EftDf8/?name=image001.png)

comments.body:
Hello Abraham,

The document upload is successful when using the `documents/v1/etds/upload` API.

You are able to see the document because it was uploaded using this API.

However, when we attempt to upload documents using `/documents/v1/etds/multiupload`, the upload consistently fails.

This is a critical issue, as the merchant processes a high volume of shipments daily and relies on this functionality.

The request and response payload I shared earlier is for /multiupload.

Could you please assist us in identifying and resolving the issue?

Regards,
Veena
Team PluginHive
